concerns.text: Tasks T004 and T012 reference dataset 'ds000246', while the spec (FR-001, US1) mandates 'ds000115'. This is a silent relaxation of the spec's data source requirement. The task list must align with the spec's FR-001 (or the spec must be updated to match the plan's correction) to satisfy the Single Source of Truth principle.

Tasks T021 and T022 implement a 'one-sample t-test', but the spec (FR-004) explicitly mandates a 'Group-Level paired-sample t-test'. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical methodology requirement. The task must either implement the paired-sample test as specified or the spec must be updated to reflect the corrected methodology before tasks are finalized.

Task T013 mandates a hard 'exit code 1' for missing event labels. The spec (US1, Acceptance Scenario 1) only requires 'file integrity' and 'BIDS structure' checks, and the Edge Cases section mentions 'fail gracefully' but does not explicitly mandate a hard stop with exit code 1 for missing labels. This introduces a stricter constraint than the spec defines, which may violate the 'no silent constitution drift' principle if the spec intended a warning-only approach.

Task T024 handles the 'no clusters survive FDR' edge case by saving an uncorrected map. The spec (Edge Cases) requires this, but the spec's Success Criteria (SC-002) states 'At least one brain cluster survives... OR the global t-statistic p-value is < 0.05'. The task does not explicitly include the logic to calculate and report the 'global t-statistic p-value' as an alternative success condition, potentially weakening the SC-002 verification step.
